Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

projects.title: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

marks_distribution: Unit 4 (d and f Block Elements): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No overall choice in the question paper
33% internal choices given in all sections
competency_weightage: Remembering and Understanding: Exhibit memory of previously learned material, recall facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Analysing, Evaluating and Creating: Examine and break information into parts, identify motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence. Present and defend opinions based on criteria. Compile information in a new pattern.
internal_assessment: 33% internal choices in all sections of the question paper
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates
practical_assessment: Written test for Visually Challenged Learners: 30 minutes, 15 questions (answer any 10)
Visually Challenged Evaluation Scheme: Identification/Familiarity (5), Written test (10), Practical Record (5), Viva (10)
project_assessment: Project Work: 04 Marks
Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources

Suggested Projects include: Study of oxalate ions in guava, quantity of casein in milk, preparation of soybean milk, effect of Potassium Bisulphate, digestion of starch by salivary amylase, fermentation rate study, extraction of essential oils, food adulterants study
Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with teacher approval

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding colloidal states, adsorption phenomena, and preparation of sols.
competencies: Distinguish between true solutions, colloids, and suspensions
Explain physisorption and chemisorption mechanisms
Identify factors affecting adsorption of gases on solids
Prepare lyophilic sols (starch, egg albumin, gum)
Prepare lyophobic sols (ferric hydroxide, aluminum hydroxide)
Demonstrate the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsions

competency_groups.title: Chromatography and Separation Techniques
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on separation methods using paper chromatography and determination of Rf values.
competencies: Separate pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers using paper chromatography
Calculate and determine Rf values for separated components
Separate constituents of inorganic mixtures based on Rf differences

competency_groups.title: Functional Group Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving identification of functional groups in organic compounds through characteristic tests.
competencies: Identify alcoholic and carboxylic groups
Identify aldehyde and ketonic groups
Perform tests for unsaturation, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, and amino groups

competency_groups.title: Food Chemistry and Biomolecule Testing
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to detecting carbohydrates, proteins, fats, and food adulterants.
competencies: Perform characteristic tests for carbohydrates in foodstuffs
Perform characteristic tests for proteins in foodstuffs
Detect common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric, chili powder, and pepper

competency_groups.title: Inorganic Compound Preparation
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the synthesis of specific inorganic compounds.
competencies: Prepare Potash Alum (double salt of Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate)
Prepare Potassium Ferric Oxalate

competency_groups.title: Quantitative Analysis and Titration
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on volumetric analysis, standard solution preparation, and molarity determination.
competencies: Prepare standard solutions of Oxalic acid
Determine molarity of KMnO4 solution using Oxalic acid
Conduct titration exercises using Ferrous ammonium sulphate (Mohr's salt)

competency_groups.title: Qualitative Analysis of Salts
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to identifying cations and anions in given salt samples.
competencies: Determine one anion from the list (CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4, CH3COO, C2O4)
Determine one cation from the list (Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4)

competency_groups.title: Organic Compound Synthesis
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the preparation of specific organic compounds.
competencies: Prepare Acetanilide
Prepare Di-benzal acetone
Prepare p-Nitroacetanilide
Prepare Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye

competency_groups.title: Investigatory Project Execution
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to conducting scientific investigations, laboratory testing, and data collection.
competencies: Study the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Prepare soybean milk and compare with natural milk regarding curd formation and temperature effects
Study the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as a food preservative under various conditions
Study digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature
Conduct comparative study of fermentation rates in wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice
Extract essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study common food adulterants

competency_groups.title: Cognitive Domain Application
competency_groups.description: Competencies reflecting the cognitive levels required for assessment (Remembering, Understanding, Applying, Analysing, Evaluating, Creating).
competencies: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts and concepts
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing and interpreting them
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge
Examine and break information into parts to identify motives or causes
Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations
Present and defend opinions based on set criteria

marks_distribution: Unit 4 (d and f Block Elements): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No overall choice in the question paper
33% internal choices given in all sections
competency_weightage: Remembering and Understanding: Exhibit memory of previously learned material, recall facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Analysing, Evaluating and Creating: Examine and break information into parts, identify motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence. Present and defend opinions based on criteria. Compile information in a new pattern.
internal_assessment: 33% internal choices in all sections of the question paper
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates
practical_assessment: Written test for Visually Challenged Learners: 30 minutes, 15 questions (answer any 10)
Visually Challenged Evaluation Scheme: Identification/Familiarity (5), Written test (10), Practical Record (5), Viva (10)
project_assessment: Project Work: 04 Marks
Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources

Suggested Projects include: Study of oxalate ions in guava, quantity of casein in milk, preparation of soybean milk, effect of Potassium Bisulphate, digestion of starch by salivary amylase, fermentation rate study, extraction of essential oils, food adulterants study
Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with teacher approval
